content.claim: Local government financing of Hungarian primary education deteriorated from central government covering 80-90% of costs in the 1990s to 50-70% (50% in small village schools) by the 2000s. This fiscal squeeze predates and is compounding current cohort decline, creating cumulative fiscal pressure independent of SEN identification trends.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Does not project forward to 2025+; more recent financing data needed to assess current local viability
Does not quantify how fiscal pressure translates into reduced specialist services or quality
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
